questions.q: Which warrior wielded the celestial bow Gandiva throughout the Kurukshetra war?
questions.explanation: Arjuna received the Gandiva at the burning of the Khandava forest and carried it through all eighteen days of the war.

questions.q: What kind of weapon was the Gandiva?
questions.explanation: The Gandiva was a celestial bow, the most famous of all the bows in the Mahabharata.

questions.q: Which god physically handed the Gandiva over for Arjuna's use?
questions.explanation: Varuna, lord of the waters, gave the Gandiva at Agni's request before the burning of the Khandava forest.

questions.q: At which event did Arjuna acquire the Gandiva?
options: The burning of the Khandava forest
The tournament of the Kuru princes
questions.explanation: Agni arranged for Arjuna to receive the Gandiva so the archer could hold off the gods while the fire god devoured the Khandava forest.

questions.q: Who is traditionally credited with creating the Gandiva?
questions.explanation: The epic describes the Gandiva as a bow of Brahma's making that passed through divine hands before reaching Arjuna.

questions.q: What came along with the Gandiva when Arjuna received it?
questions.explanation: Arjuna received two quivers that could never be emptied, ensuring he was never without arrows in battle.

questions.q: To what was the twang of the Gandiva famously compared?
questions.explanation: The Gandiva's twang sounded like thunder and struck terror into enemy armies across the battlefield.

questions.q: Which god arranged for Arjuna to get the Gandiva so that the Khandava forest could be consumed?
questions.explanation: Agni, weakened and hungry, asked Varuna to give Arjuna the Gandiva so the archer could fight off Indra's rains while the forest burned.

questions.q: What finally happened to the Gandiva at the end of the Pandavas' story?
options: It was cast back into the waters
questions.explanation: On the final journey Arjuna threw the Gandiva and its quivers into the waters, returning them to Varuna.

questions.q: Which of Arjuna's famous names comes directly from the bow he carried?
questions.explanation: Gandivadhanva means the wielder of the Gandiva bow, one of Arjuna's many epithets.

questions.q: Where was the Gandiva hidden during the Pandavas' year in disguise?
options: Under the floor of Virata's palace
Wrapped like a corpse in a shami tree
questions.explanation: The Pandavas wrapped their weapons to resemble a corpse and hung the bundle in a shami tree so no one would touch it.

questions.q: Whom did Arjuna nearly kill because of an insult spoken against the Gandiva?
questions.explanation: Arjuna had vowed to kill anyone who told him to give the Gandiva away, and Yudhishthira's angry taunt invoked that very vow.

questions.q: What did Yudhishthira say in anger that triggered Arjuna's terrible vow about the Gandiva?
options: That Arjuna should hand the bow to a worthier man
That the bow had been won by deceit
That Karna's Vijaya was the greater bow
That the bow had failed the Pandavas
questions.explanation: Smarting from his defeat by Karna, Yudhishthira told Arjuna to give the Gandiva to someone else, the one insult Arjuna had sworn to answer with death.

questions.q: How did Krishna defuse Arjuna's vow to kill anyone who insulted the Gandiva?
options: He took the sin upon himself
He made Yudhishthira withdraw the words
He advised Arjuna to address his elder harshly, which counts as killing him
He reminded Arjuna the vow was made in childhood
questions.explanation: Krishna taught that speaking disrespectfully to an elder is equivalent to slaying him, so harsh words could satisfy the vow without bloodshed.

questions.q: After harsh words satisfied his vow, what did Arjuna do to symbolically kill himself in atonement?
options: He fasted for a full day
He praised his own deeds aloud
He broke one of his arrows
questions.explanation: Krishna explained that self-praise is a form of self-destruction, so Arjuna boasted of himself to atone for threatening his brother.

questions.q: Along with the Gandiva at Khandava, what else did Arjuna receive?
options: A divine chariot yoked to white horses
questions.explanation: Arjuna also received a celestial chariot with white steeds that bore the monkey banner, all delivered at Agni's request.

questions.q: In the opening of the Bhagavad Gita, what does Arjuna say is happening to the Gandiva?
options: It is slipping from his hand
It has grown too heavy to lift
questions.explanation: Overcome at the sight of his kinsmen arrayed for slaughter, Arjuna says his body trembles and the Gandiva slips from his hand.

questions.q: How does the epic measure the Gandiva's power against ordinary weapons?
options: Equal to a hundred thousand bows
questions.explanation: In the Virata Parva the Gandiva is declared equal to a hundred thousand bows in strength.

questions.q: After Krishna's death, against whom did the Gandiva fail in Arjuna's hands?
options: The Abhira robbers who attacked the Yadava women
The remnants of the Kaurava host
questions.explanation: Escorting the Yadava women after Dwarka's fall, Arjuna found his strength and weapon-craft gone when Abhira robbers raided the caravan.

questions.q: On the final journey, who appeared before the Pandavas to demand the Gandiva's return?
questions.explanation: Agni rose before the departing Pandavas as a wall of fire and told Arjuna to cast the bow and quivers into the waters, for their purpose was complete.

questions.q: During the cattle raid on Virata, who climbed the shami tree to bring down the wrapped weapons including the Gandiva?
questions.explanation: At Brihannala's direction the young prince Uttara climbed the tree and unwrapped the Pandavas' weapons before the battle with the Kurus.

questions.q: What feat did the Gandiva make possible at the burning of the Khandava forest?
options: Holding off Indra's rains and host while Agni fed
questions.explanation: With the Gandiva Arjuna wove a canopy of arrows that blocked Indra's protecting rains so Agni could consume the forest.

questions.q: According to Arjuna's account in the Virata Parva, who held the Gandiva first, for a full thousand years?
questions.explanation: Arjuna recounts that Brahma held the bow first for a thousand years before it passed to Prajapati, Indra, Soma, and Varuna.

questions.q: Which deity held the Gandiva immediately before Varuna received it?
questions.explanation: In the bow's lineage of bearers, Soma held the Gandiva for five hundred years before passing it to Varuna.

questions.q: What does the epic claim about the Gandiva's durability?
options: Only Shiva's trident could scratch it
Neither gods nor demons could break it
It could be strung only at night
questions.explanation: The Gandiva is described as indestructible, capable of withstanding gods and danavas alike.

questions.q: On which day of the Kurukshetra war did the crisis over the insult to the Gandiva take place?
questions.explanation: On the seventeenth day, after Karna humiliated Yudhishthira in battle, the wounded king's taunt about the Gandiva nearly turned Arjuna against him.

questions.q: In which book of the Mahabharata does Arjuna nearly slay his brother over the Gandiva?
questions.explanation: The episode of the insulted Gandiva and Krishna's saving counsel occurs in the Karna Parva, the eighth book of the epic.

questions.q: To whom did Arjuna recount the full list of the Gandiva's divine bearers?
questions.explanation: While retrieving the weapons from the shami tree, Arjuna told Prince Uttara how the bow had passed from god to god before reaching him.

questions.q: After the Gandiva failed against the Abhira robbers, who told Arjuna that the weapons' appointed time had passed?
questions.explanation: The grieving Arjuna sought out Vyasa, who explained that the weapons and their wielders had fulfilled their purpose and the era was over.

questions.q: For how many years does Arjuna say he himself had wielded the Gandiva?
questions.explanation: In the Virata Parva Arjuna lists the bow's bearers and says Partha held it for sixty-five years after Varuna's hundred.
